steps.text: Consult either the air permitting authority for the entity or your EPA Regional representative as listed in 40 CFR 63.13.
File a petition for review in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

preconditions.text: If you have any questions regarding the applicability of this action to a particular entity
If seeking judicial review of this final rule

constraints.text: The petition must be filed by May 30, 2023.
Only an objection to this final rule that was raised with reasonable specificity during the period for public comment can be raised during judicial review.

steps.text: Perform leak checks during a sampling run in accordance with § 60.5476(f)(2).
Perform post-test leak check procedures in accordance with § 60.5476(f)(1).

constraints.text: Do not average the PM results from a non-leaking sampling system with those from a leaking sampling system, as this renders the test method inappropriate for compliance determination.

steps.text: Perform leak checks using the procedure in paragraph (c)(1) if conducting them immediately after component changes.

Measure the first hour of particulate matter emissions for each test run by sampling with a third, identical and independent sampling train operated concurrently for the first hour of PM paired train compliance testing.
Report the test results from the third train separately as the first hour emissions.

preconditions.text: The test is conducted using ASTM E2515–11 (with exceptions described in paragraphs (c)(1) and (2) of this section).

constraints.text: Immediately after component changes, leak checks are optional but highly recommended.

steps.text: Determine the location and layout of the traverse points following the procedure outlined in section 11.3 and table 1-1 or 1-2, using a minimum of 40 traverse points for circular ducts and 42 points for rectangular ducts.
Use a directional flow-sensing probe to measure pitch and yaw angles of the gas flow at the traverse points.
Calculate the resultant angle and compare with acceptable criteria for mean and standard deviation.
Use the same minimum of 40 traverse points for circular ducts and 42 points for rectangular ducts for future sampling and velocity measurements.

preconditions.text: Measurement locations are less than 2 equivalent or duct diameters downstream or less than one-half duct diameter upstream from a flow disturbance.
Ducts are larger than 24 inches in diameter where blockage and wall effects are minimal.
The alternative measurement location is determined to be acceptable according to the criteria in this alternative procedure.

constraints.text: Both the pitch and yaw angles must be measured from a line passing through the traverse point and parallel to the stack axis. The pitch angle is in the plane that includes the traverse line and is parallel to the stack axis. The yaw angle is in the plane perpendicular to the traverse line at the traverse point.

steps.text: Calculate the carbon monoxide emissions rate (g/min) for each minute of the test period.
Calculate total CO emissions for each of the four test periods (CO_1, CO_2, CO_3, CO_4) as the sum of the emissions rates for each of the 1-minute intervals.
Calculate total CO emissions for the test run, COT, as the sum of CO_1, CO_2, CO_3 and CO_4.
Initiate a quality program following the requirements of ASTM D6216–20, section 8.
Include a quality system in the quality program.
Include a corrective action program in the quality program.
Conduct the CD test at the two points specified in section 6.1.2 of this method.
Introduce to the CEMS the reference gases, gas cells, or optical filters.
Record the CEMS response.
Subtract this value from the reference value.
Calculate the RA, expressed as a percentage, of a set of data.

preconditions.text: When using reference gases, introduce the reference gas prior to any sample conditioning or filtration equipment and ensure that it passes through all filters, scrubbers, conditioners, and other monitor components used during normal sampling.

In cases where the average emissions for the test are less than 50 percent of the applicable emission standard, substitute the applicable emission standard value in the denominator of equation 2–6 of this method in place of the average RM value. In all other cases, use RM.

constraints.text: The reference gas should pass through as much of the sampling probe as practical.

steps.text: Consult the CEMS user's manual for specific precautions to be taken with regard to the analytical procedures.

preconditions.text: Establish appropriate safety and health practices and determine the applicable regulatory limitations prior to performing this performance specification.

constraints.text: This performance specification may involve hazardous materials, operations, and equipment.
For O2, the span must be 25 percent, or higher if the O2 concentration at the sampling point can be greater than 25 percent.
For CO, the low-range span must be 200 ppm and the high range span must be 3000 ppm.
The scale for both CEMS must record all readings within a measurement range with a resolution of 0.5 percent.

steps.text: Calculate the CERMS Relative Accuracy (RA) expressed as a percentage using equation 2–6 of section 12 of PS 2.
Use method 29, method 30A, or method 30B in appendix A–8 to this part or ASTM Method D6784–16 as the RM for Hg concentration.
Conduct the RM test runs with paired or duplicate sampling.

preconditions.text: If the average emissions for the test are less than 50 percent of the applicable emission standard, you may elect to substitute the applicable emission standard value in the denominator of equation 2–6 in place of the average RM value.
Unless otherwise specified in an applicable subpart of this part
When method 29, method 30B, or ASTM D6784–16 is used

constraints.text: The RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 20.0 percent in terms of the units of the emission standard (or no greater than 10.0 percent if the average emission standard value is substituted).
For method 29 and ASTM Method D6784–16 only, the filterable portion of the sample need not be included when making comparisons to the CEMS results.

steps.text: Determine RD for paired data points.
Flag data that do not meet the RD criteria as a data quality problem.
Do not use flagged data in the calculation of RA.

preconditions.text: If data do not meet the RD criteria

constraints.text: The RA of the CEMS must be no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data in terms of units of mg/scm. Alternatively, if the mean RM is less than 2.5 mg/scm, the absolute value of the difference between the mean RM and CEMS values added to the absolute value of the confidence coefficient from equation 12A–7 must not exceed 0.5 mg/scm.

steps.text: Conduct the applicable ongoing tests listed in this section.
Filter the sample through the filter paper, into an Erlenmeyer flask by applying a vacuum to the flask sidearm.
Replace filter paper as often as needed in order to maintain filter times of less than approximately 30 seconds per filter paper.
Perform method 5210B on the filtrate obtained.
Seed dilution water with 1 milliliter of final effluent per liter of dilution water.
Use three BOD bottles and different dilutions for each sample.

constraints.text: Minimize the time for which vacuum is applied to prevent stripping of volatile organics from the sample.
No rinsing of sample container or filter bowl into the Erlenmeyer flask is allowed.
Dilution ratios may require adjustment to reflect the lower oxygen demand of the filtered sample in comparison to the total BOD5.

steps.text: Measure THC at the inlet and the outlet of the control device.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the exhaust of the stationary RICE.
Determine the O2 concentration of the stationary RICE exhaust at the sampling port location.

preconditions.text: If demonstrating compliance with the THC percent reduction requirement

For formaldehyde, CO, O2, and moisture measurement, ducts ≤6 inches in diameter may be sampled at a single point located at the duct centroid and ducts >6 and ≤12 inches in diameter may be sampled at 3 traverse points located at 16.7, 50.0, and 83.3% of the measurement line (‘3-point long line’). If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A, the duct may be sampled at ‘3-point long line’; otherwise, conduct the stratification testing and select sampling points according to section 8.1.2 of method 7E of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A. If using a control device, the sampling site must be located at the outlet of the control device.

constraints.text: Formaldehyde concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.
THC concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.
Method 25A, reported as propane, of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–7.
Method 3 or 3A or 3B of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–2, or ASTM D6522–00 (Reapproved 2005) 1 3 (heated probe not necessary).

steps.text: Perform test runs directly comparing CEMS measurements to the reference method.
Complete the RATA.
Report all data, including any rejected data.

constraints.text: A minimum of nine valid test runs must be performed.

If more than nine test runs are performed, a maximum of three test runs may be rejected so long as the total number of test results used to determine the relative accuracy is greater than or equal to nine.
If method 30A is used, the minimum time per run is 21 minutes.
If method 29, method 30B, or ASTM D6784–16 is used, the time per run must be long enough to collect a sufficient mass of Hg to analyze.
Complete the RATA within 168 unit operating hours, except when method 29 or ASTM D6784–162 is used, in which case, up to 336 operating hours may be taken to finish the test.
